14.reason: [When the input is "0.0", this leads to undefined behavior because s[i] would be '\0', and printing it as %c is invalid. Also, for inputs like "0.1", s[i+1] might be '\n' or '\0', leading to incorrect output format.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When the input is "0.0", this leads to undefined behavior because s[i] would be '\0', and printing it as %c is invalid. Also, for inputs like "0.1", s[i+1] might be '\n' or '\0', leading to incorrect output format.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When the input is "0.0", this accesses s[i] where i goes beyond valid digits, leading to undefined behavior. Also, if input ends right after a non-zero digit (e.g., "0.1"), s[i+1] is '\n' or '\0', but logic assumes presence of more digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When the input is "0.0", this accesses s[i] where i goes beyond valid digits, leading to undefined behavior. Also, for inputs like "0.1", s[i+1] may be '\n' or '\0', causing incorrect format.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When the input is "0.0", this accesses s[i] where i goes beyond valid digits, leading to undefined behavior. Also, if input ends with newline from fgets, s[i+1] might be '\n' not '\0', causing incorrect format.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [If the input is "0.0", this will access s[i] where i goes beyond valid digits, leading to undefined behavior. Also, it doesn't handle case where all digits after decimal are zero properly.]

30.reason: [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, which results in extra characters (like '\n') in the output. It should print only up to the decimal point or remove the newline first.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, which results in extra characters (like '\n') in the output. It should print only up to the decimal point or remove the newline first.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, and also includes the decimal point (e.g., for input "5.", it prints "5.e0" instead of "5e0" or "5.0e0". Also, per problem spec, if only one digit before decimal and it's non-zero, output should be just that digit without decimal unless there are fractional digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, and also includes the decimal point (e.g., "5.e0" instead of "5e0" or "5.0e0" as required). Additionally, it fails to remove the trailing newline from fgets input, which corrupts output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, and also includes the decimal point (e.g., "5.e0" instead of "5e0"). It should print only the first digit without the decimal point when there's only one digit before '.']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the entire string including the newline character read by fgets, and also includes the decimal point (e.g., "5.e0" instead of "5e0"). It should print only the first digit without the decimal point for single-digit integer cases.]
